column13: Hopla! is a vibrant circus festival held in Brussels, Belgium. It features a variety of circus performances, street art, and music, celebrating the city's rich cultural heritage. The festival aims to connect circus artists with the people of Brussels, fostering wonder, curiosity, and self-transcendence through various performances. Throughout the week, Hopla! brings shows to different neighborhoods in Brussels, while the weekend features performances in central locations such as Vismet, Place Sainte-Catherine, Place du Béguinage, and Square Marguerite Duras. The event is largely free, though some indoor performances may require reservations.

column3: The Miramiro Summer Festival 2025 promises a spectacular edition filled with circus performances from the Ghent circus workshop, offering numerous shows for all ages. The festival will feature a variety of performances and entertainment, including music and circus acts, making it a vibrant and exciting event for attendees of all ages.

column13: Namur en Mai is a vibrant street festival held annually in the city of Namur, Belgium. It features a variety of cultural performances, street art, music, and local cuisine, attracting visitors from all over the country and beyond. The festival includes acrobatics, juggling, dance, puppet shows, and a mix of traditional and innovative performances.

The Summer of Antwerp (Zomer van Antwerpen) is a vibrant street festival featuring theater, music, circus, and film performances in unexpected locations throughout the city. It includes a mix of local and international performances, with highlights such as 'Falstaff,' 'Efectos Especiales,' and 'The Conference of the Birds.' The festival is accessible to all, with additional materials for wheelchair users and the deaf community.

The Chassepierre Festival is an annual International Festival of Street Arts that transforms the village of Chassepierre into a vibrant hub for street theater and circus performances. It features a diverse lineup of professional companies from the circus and street arts sectors, attracting artists and spectators from across Europe and beyond.

The Festival UP Belgium 2025 is part of the 31st season of UP – Circus & Performing Arts, which has been supporting contemporary circus for 26 years in Brussels, Belgium, and internationally. The festival includes various events and performances focused on circus arts and performing arts.

La Piste aux Espoirs is an international contemporary circus festival featuring a variety of performances, including street shows, innovative creations, and family-friendly activities. The festival collaborates with Maison de la Culture de Tournai and offers workshops, games, and networking opportunities for professionals and the public.

Berlin Lacht is an international street theater festival that brings together extraordinary artists from various nations to perform in Berlin. The festival features comedy acts, live music, and street performances, celebrating humor and entertainment in the heart of Berlin. The 2025 edition will take place at two main locations: Potsdamer Platz (August 5 to August 24) and Washingtonplatz (Berlin Hauptbahnhof) (August 26 to September 15).

The Soundance Festival is known for its unique blend of dance, music, and text, offering a variety of performances that are choreographed, improvised, instrumental, live, or recorded. Each performance is distinct in its artistic style, making the festival a standout event for enthusiasts of contemporary dance and music.

The Artlake Festival is an annual event held in Germany that combines music, art, and workshops. It is known for its diverse array of cultural activities and has become a favorite among music and art enthusiasts. The festival typically features live bands, DJs, workshops, and various forms of artistic expression, creating a vibrant and creative atmosphere. It is celebrated for its community spirit and the opportunity it provides for attendees to actively participate in various art forms. The festival is held in scenic locations, often near lakes, which adds to the overall experience.

The Theaterformen festival in Germany for 2025 features a variety of contemporary productions by international artists, including performances, lectures, documentaries, musicals, and choreographed excursions into nature. Notable performances include 'A Year Without Summer' and 'Los días afuera / The days out there' by Lola Arias. The festival also includes a Festival Campus Niedersachsen for students and emerging artists.

The Brighton Festival in the United Kingdom for 2025 promises to be an exciting and diverse celebration of the arts, featuring a wide range of events across various art forms, including music, theatre, dance, circus, visual arts, film, literature, and debate. The festival will be curated by Grammy-nominated musician and composer Anoushka Shankar, inspired by the theme of ‘New Dawn’.

The Spettacolo Brunnen is an international street artist festival held in Brunnen, Switzerland. This biennial event features over 50 street artists from around the world, transforming the Brunnen lakeside promenade into a vibrant cultural spectacle with performances and a food mile.
A contemporary dance and performance festival with a theme of 'Signs of Resistance.' It aims to explore how movement can reframe boundaries and includes performances by various artists.

The Buskers Chur festival transforms the alpine city of Chur into a vibrant cultural hub under the open sky, featuring over 70 international artists from 20 different nations. The event offers a diverse program blending music, theater, circus acts, and artistic performances with more than 200 shows over two days. Attendees are encouraged to purchase a festival wristband and contribute 'hat money' for the performances.

column13: The Dynamo Circus Festival in Denmark celebrates contemporary circus arts, featuring acrobatics, juggling, clowning, puppet shows, live performances, music, workshops, and film screenings. The festival includes events such as live concerts, open stage performances, and late-night afterparties. It also offers open circus training at the Circus Playground and a film competition called '60secondscircusfilm'.

A six-day event featuring a variety of performances including dance, contemporary circus, comedy, and visual performing arts. The festival hosts 19 performing arts companies from 13 different countries, making it one of Denmark's largest curated international performing arts festivals.

The KLP Festival is the largest self-organized cultural festival in Northern Germany, held annually between Ascension Day and Pentecost. It features live music, local food stalls, art exhibitions, and various cultural performances at multiple locations, including the Gasthof Meuchefitz.

column2: Dance In The City - Festival Tanzende Stadt

column13: The 'Bamberg Zaubert' festival is an annual event held in Bamberg, Germany, that transforms the historic city center into a vibrant stage for international street and variety performances. It features over 500 performances across approximately 20 stages, showcasing a diverse range of acts including magic, acrobatics, juggling, fire and light shows, and music. It attracts around 250,000 visitors each year and includes performances by over 100 international artists.

The Düsseldorf Festival, which began in 1991 as Altstadtherbst, has grown into a significant event featuring a variety of performing arts including music, dance, theater, and new circus. The festival presents international productions and performances at various venues across the city, including Burgplatz.

The Neuland Winter-Varieté features a new program combining air acrobatics, artistry, juggling, humor, dance, and live singing, with performers from various countries including Japan, Germany, Italy, Denmark, Switzerland, France, and the USA. The show is approximately two hours long, including an intermission, and is suitable for all ages.
Festival for contemporary circus with various cultural events, workshops, and performances.

The Brückensensationen Festival in Rheinfelden, Germany, is a vibrant street art festival that takes place annually. The festival is known for its unique setting around the old Rhine Bridge, which connects Switzerland and Germany, turning the area into a large open-air stage. The event features a variety of performances, including street theater, music, and other artistic displays, attracting around 12,000 visitors each year. The festival typically spans three days and includes numerous food and drink stalls, adding to the festive atmosphere.

The Frei Art Festival 2025 is an international dance and culture festival featuring performances, workshops, and seminars. It includes a variety of contemporary and urban dance sessions led by renowned artists, special events with short pieces by international choreographers, and residencies for selected artists.

The Hut Festival is a vibrant street festival held in Chemnitz, Germany. It features a variety of street performances, live music, local food stalls, and art exhibitions. The festival celebrates the cultural diversity and creativity of the region, attracting both locals and tourists.

column4: Mülheim An Der Ruhr, Cologne, Düsseldorf
column13: A significant platform for independent theater in the German-speaking world, featuring showcase productions, the 'Post-West' programming strand, and a range of discursive formats, workshops, and parties. The 2025 edition explores the theme 'Gottes Garten' (God's Garden) and examines aspects of a post-Western society.

The ViaThea Street Theater Festival is an international festival held annually in the cities of Görlitz and Zgorzelec. It transforms the streets and squares into an open-air stage, offering a diverse program of theater, dance, performance art, circus, and other art forms. The festival presents traditional and experimental theater and dance forms, performance art, contemporary circus, audio walks, and participatory projects in public spaces. It emphasizes togetherness, accessibility, and integration, with special accessibility information and a 'Rolli Podest' supported by the 'Barrier-Free Construction' investment program.

The Lesliehof in Graz hosts several exciting events in 2025, including the Leslie Open Air Cinema from August 11 to 30, featuring international classics and Austrian films, and the Grazer ARTvent Markt, an advent market from November 28 to December 23, showcasing artisanal crafts and culinary delights.

The Kultursommer Wien (Vienna Summer of Culture) transforms various locations across Vienna into open-air stages, offering a diverse program of free cultural events. The festival features cabaret, literature, music, dance, theater, and contemporary circus, with special offerings like family programs and participatory workshops. The 2025 edition runs under the motto 'Culture is where you are' and includes over 2,000 artists and 500 acts across 28 venues in 17 districts.

column6: Diane Fleury: diane@belluard.ch Gionata Morganti: gionata@belluard.ch

column13: The Villach Street Art Festival transforms the city center into a vibrant stage for approximately 100 international street artists from ten different nations. Attendees can expect a diverse program featuring jugglers, mimes, acrobats, musicians, comedians, and fire-eaters who perform as dusk settles. Performances will be held in various central locations, including Hauptplatz, Rathausplatz, and Kirchenplatz. The festival is free to attend, and artists typically rely on donations from the audience.

An international street theater and circus festival held annually in Lienz, Austria. The festival features street art, acrobatics, comedy, juggling, interactive productions, music, and colorful walking acts. The 2025 edition includes a metaphysical circus performance and the Olala midday parade on Saturday.

The Linzer Pflasterspektakel is an international street art festival featuring acrobatics, juggling, dance, comedy, clowning, pantomime, magic, figure and object theatre, high-wire and fire artistry, and music. Approximately 108 international artists and companies from 34 countries perform around 800 shows across 40 venues in the city center. Special features include the Kaleidoskop-Revue and musical nightlines in city center pubs, as well as a Children's Spectacle with expanded activities in the Donaupark.

column2: Ana Desetnica - International Street Art Festival

column13: Circusstad Rotterdam 2025 is a vibrant hub of contemporary circus arts featuring five days of spectacular performances, workshops, and interactive experiences. The festival blends traditional circus elements with modern artistic expressions like dance, theater, and music. Highlights include international acts, outdoor performances, and inclusive projects like 'Buitengewoon.' The festival takes place at various locations across Rotterdam, including Schouwburgplein, Theater Rotterdam, and the old Luxor Theater.

The Cirqu'Arau festival in Switzerland focuses on contemporary circus arts, featuring a variety of performances from small experimental forms to family shows. It takes place in various venues in Aarau, including the Alte Reithalle, museums, and tents.

The 31st International Festival of Street Artists in Vevey, Switzerland, featuring jugglers, mimes, actors, acrobats, fire-eaters, and clowns from around the world. Performances are held in the Old Town of Vevey, including the Place Scanavin, with a competition for the 'Pavé d'or' prize.
An international theater festival held at the Landiwiese by Lake Zurich, transforming the area into a unique setting for performance and theater.
An event focused on art, culture, and history, featuring live concerts, a fashion show, a gastronomic market, and a cocktail bar.

A smaller, one-day event called Spoffin Spin-Off featuring approximately thirteen street theatre acts, primarily from the Netherlands, held at various locations in and around the city center of Amersfoort. The event is freely accessible, with visitors having the option to decide what they pay after each show, and all proceeds going directly to the artists. The event concludes with a fire performance.

The Festival d'Aurillac, also known as the International Street Theatre Festival, will hold its 38th edition in 2025. The festival will spotlight Brazilian creation as part of the 'Saison du Brésil en France,' featuring around 19 official companies and over 650 independent visiting companies. It draws more than 130,000 attendees over its four days.

The Festival d'Avignon 2025, including both the 'In' and 'Off' festivals, transforms Avignon into a global hub for contemporary performing arts. The 79th edition features theatre, dance, music, and multidisciplinary works across various iconic venues. Arabic is the guest language, and choreographer Marlene Monteiro Freitas will open the festival.

The 'Grande Finale' of the international circus festival Young Stage Basel, celebrating nearly 20 years of the festival's history. It showcases young, modern circus artistry with top-class acts from twelve countries, selected from 640 applications across 66 nations. The event includes main shows, workshops, and panel discussions, serving as a significant platform for young circus artists to launch their careers.

column13: The Bibasse 2025 juggling convention, the 11th edition of the event, is a significant event in the French flow arts and juggling scene. Attendees can expect a vibrant program including circus shows, concerts, workshops, fire jams, and juggling games.

column13: The 'ARé' Performing Arts Festival, also known as ARé Fest 2025, is dedicated to exploring the dynamic intersections between performing and visual arts, embracing experimental and unconventional forms of expression. The festival's program features performances, visual installations, discussions, and workshops across various venues in Yerevan. It aims to bring together international and local artists, foster global partnerships, and serve as a hub for creative exchange, showcasing Armenia's contemporary art scene.

The Athens Epidaurus Festival is one of Greece's most significant cultural events, offering a diverse program of ancient Greek drama, music, contemporary dance, and interdisciplinary performances. It takes place at various venues including the Ancient Theatre of Epidaurus, the Little Theatre of Ancient Epidaurus, the Odeon of Herodes Atticus in Athens, and Peiraios 260.

The Attendorn Street Performers Festival transforms the town center into a stage for comedy, music, acrobatics, and interactive activities. It features international street artists and musicians, attracting thousands of visitors annually. The festival includes performances for children, families, and all ages, culminating in the awarding of the 'KleinKunstPreis'.

The TNT Festival Terrassa Noves Tendències is an 18th edition of the festival of live arts and new forms, dedicated to promoting and disseminating contemporary and multidisciplinary creation. It supports critical and committed artistic practices, embracing new artistic languages and exploring new ways of engaging with artistic expression. The festival addresses contemporary issues such as ecology, inequality, gender, diversity, and the dominance of new technologies.

Theaterfestival Basel is a biennial event featuring various theater performances across multiple venues in Basel. The 2025 edition includes a collaboration with Kaserne Basel, taking place at the Kasernenareal and other partner theaters.

The Feel Festival at Berghaider See is a four-day cultural event featuring a diverse range of activities, including music, art, workshops, and discussions on topics such as sustainability and queer feminism. The festival offers karaoke, yoga sessions, saunas, and various light and art installations. Musically, it spans genres like electronic music, Indie, Rock, Punk, and Hip-hop.

The Buskers Bern Street Music Festival transforms the streets of Bern's Old Town into a vibrant open-air festival. The 22nd edition features approximately 150 artists from around the world, showcasing music, dance, theatre, comedy, street performance, puppet theatre, art, and artistry. Around 70,000 visitors attend annually. Admission is free, but attendees can support the non-profit event by purchasing program booklets and festival wristbands.

column13: The largest street art festival in Eastern Switzerland, featuring over 100 artists from 26 nations presenting around 400 shows, including acrobatics, music, comedy, and dance, along with a diverse selection of street food.

The Asfaltart International Street Art Festival transforms Merano into a vibrant open-air arena with over 200 performances by clowns, acrobats, dancers, fire-breathers, jugglers, tightrope walkers, and musicians from around the world. The festival includes events in Schenna and Dorf Tirol, with a parade, fire shows, high-wire acts, street theater, and culinary delights. It is a family-friendly event emphasizing sustainability as an official GreenEvent.
